---
title: "UIAO Modernization Master Plan — Compliance & ATO Alignment"
subtitle: "Control bindings, KSI alignment, evidence chain, and the continuous ATO operating model"
doc-type: master-plan
phase: "Phase 2 — Compliance & ATO Alignment"
audience: "CIO · CISO · ISSO · Authorizing Official (AO) · 3PAO · Compliance Officers"
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml"
derived-from: "boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd"
upstream:
  - "phase-0-planning.qmd §5 Objectives & Outcomes"
  - "phase-1-architecture.qmd §5 Control Binding Model"
  - "phase-1-architecture.qmd §7 Conformance Criteria"
  - "boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd §3 Continuity Requirements"
  - "boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd §6 Conformance Criteria"
aspirational: true
date: 2026-04-26
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
---

# UIAO Modernization Master Plan — Compliance & ATO Alignment {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## Series Map — you are reading **document 4 of 5**
[Series index](index.qmd) ·
[Phase 0 Planning](phase-0-planning.qmd) →
[Phase 1 Architecture](phase-1-architecture.qmd) →
[Boundary Modernization Appendix](boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd) →
**Compliance & ATO Alignment** (this document) →
[90-Day Execution Plan](90-day-execution-plan.qmd)
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Document Purpose
This document binds the UIAO architecture to the federal compliance
landscape and defines the continuous ATO operating model. It produces
the per-mandate crosswalk approach, the KSI alignment model, the
evidence-chain requirements that authorize the cATO posture, the POA&M
model, and the AO decision-support surface. It consumes the Phase 1
Control Binding Model (§5) and Conformance Criteria (§7), and the
Boundary Modernization Appendix Continuity Requirements (§3) and
Conformance Criteria (§6).
:::

---

## 1. Mandate Landscape

UIAO is bound to a defined set of federal mandates and frameworks. The
program does not introduce net-new compliance regimes; it aligns to the
existing landscape and makes the alignment evidentially complete.

| Mandate / Framework | Authority | Scope of Alignment |
|---|---|---|
| **EO 14028** | Executive Order, May 2021 | Zero Trust transition, EDR, logging maturity, supply-chain integrity |
| **OMB M-22-09** | Office of Management and Budget | Federal Zero Trust strategy, identity and device conditions of access |
| **NIST SP 800-207** | National Institute of Standards and Technology | Zero Trust architecture reference model |
| **NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5** | NIST | Security and privacy control catalog |
| **FedRAMP Rev 5** | FedRAMP PMO | Cloud-service authorization baseline (Low / Moderate / High) |
| **CISA SCuBA** | Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency | Secure configuration baselines for M365 and cloud business apps |
| **OMB M-24-15** | OMB | Continuous monitoring and ongoing authorization expectations |
| **NIST OSCAL** | NIST | Machine-readable representation of catalogs, profiles, components, SSP, SAR, POA&M |

Alignment to each mandate is expressed as plane bindings (Phase 1 §5)
and conformance criteria (this document §7), not as narrative claims.

---

## 2. Control Binding Approach

Phase 1 §5 defines the binding shape. This section commits the program
to the binding *approach*: how controls are selected, attached to
planes, evidenced, and arbitrated.

### 2.1 Binding Pipeline

| Step | Activity | Output |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Catalog ingestion | Canonical control library populated from NIST 800-53 Rev 5, FedRAMP Rev 5 baselines, SCuBA baselines, and KSI definitions |
| 2 | Plane mapping | Each control bound to one or more planes per Phase 1 §5.1 |
| 3 | Signal selection | Each binding cites the signal class on which conformance is evidenced (Phase 1 §4) |
| 4 | Evidence pattern | Each binding declares the provenance-record shape that constitutes proof |
| 5 | Cadence assignment | Each binding declares a re-evaluation cadence consistent with cATO §5 |
| 6 | Arbitration rule | Where a control binds to multiple planes, the arbitration rule names the Compliance plane as sole arbiter |
| 7 | Gap recording | Controls with no observable signal are recorded as Compliance-plane gaps and managed through POA&M |

### 2.2 Binding Quality Bar

A binding is acceptable when, and only when:

1. The cited signal class is producible by the cited plane.
2. The evidence pattern is verifiable from the provenance graph alone,
   without out-of-band claims.
3. The cadence is achievable by the deployment without continuous human
   intervention.
4. The arbitration rule names a single canonical authority.

Bindings that fail this bar are recorded as gaps; they are not
relaxed to fit available signal.

---

## 3. KSI Alignment

Key Security Indicators (KSIs) are the program's compact, AO-facing
expression of compliance posture. They are derived from control state,
not asserted independently.

### 3.1 KSI Derivation Rule

Every KSI satisfies the following derivation chain:

```
provenance-record  →  control-state  →  KSI outcome  →  authorization-state
       (Evidence)        (Compliance)     (Compliance)      (Continuous ATO)
```

A KSI that cannot be traced to a provenance-record is not a KSI; it is
an unsupported claim and is rejected at the Compliance plane.

### 3.2 KSI Tiering

| Tier | Audience | Update Cadence | Example Signal Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Tier 1 — AO surface** | Authorizing Official | Daily or event-driven | Aggregate control posture, boundary-conformance posture |
| **Tier 2 — ISSO surface** | Information System Security Officer | Hourly | Control-family rollups, drift trend |
| **Tier 3 — Operator surface** | Engineering operators | Continuous | Per-control state, per-binding evidence freshness |

Tiering is a presentation choice; the underlying provenance is the same
across tiers.

### 3.3 KSI Drift

KSIs are themselves subject to Drift Detection. A KSI whose underlying
evidence pattern has not refreshed within its declared cadence is
flagged as *stale* — distinct from *failed* — and surfaces to the AO
with that classification.

---

## 4. Evidence Chain Requirements

The evidence chain is the single source of truth for compliance. The
following requirements are normative; deviation invalidates the cATO
posture for the affected control bindings.

### 4.1 Chain Properties

| Property | Requirement |
|---|---|
| **Attribution** | Every record names a producer (human, automation, or governance engine) |
| **Timestamp** | Every record carries a monotonic, attributable timestamp |
| **Immutability** | Records are append-only; corrections are new records that cite the corrected predecessor |
| **Reconstructability** | Any control-state assertion must be reconstructable from the chain alone |
| **Boundary-Continuity** | Every boundary crossing in the evidence path must satisfy Boundary Appendix §3.1 |
| **Schema-Conformance** | Records validate against the canonical schema; non-conforming records are quarantined, not consumed |

### 4.2 Forbidden Evidence Patterns

Three patterns are explicitly disallowed:

1. **Narrative-only evidence.** A control whose evidence is a written
   description with no underlying provenance-record is a gap, not an
   evidenced control.
2. **Sampled evidence presented as continuous.** Point-in-time samples
   may not be aggregated into continuous-monitoring claims unless the
   sampling cadence is itself recorded and conformant.
3. **Reconstruction via heuristic.** Evidence reconstructed from
   inference (rather than from canonical source) violates SRF Invariant
   2 (Boundary Appendix §4.2) and is rejected.

### 4.3 OSCAL Surface

The Compliance plane emits OSCAL artifacts as the canonical machine-
readable representation:

| Artifact | Source | Consumer |
|---|---|---|
| **OSCAL Component** | Per-plane control bindings | SSP assembly |
| **OSCAL SSP** | Aggregated component bindings + boundary description | AO, 3PAO |
| **OSCAL SAR** | Assessment results derived from provenance graph | AO, 3PAO |
| **OSCAL POA&M** | Open gaps and scheduled remediation | AO, 3PAO, ISSO |
| **OSCAL Profile** | Tailored baseline (FedRAMP Rev 5 Moderate or applicable variant) | Internal program governance |

OSCAL output is a *projection* of the canonical evidence chain, not a
parallel record. Projections must be regenerable from the chain at any
time.

---

## 5. Continuous ATO Operating Model

Continuous ATO (cATO) is the operating posture under which authorization
is sustained, not periodically renewed. The model has four mechanisms.

### 5.1 Mechanism 1 — Authorization-State Derivation

The Continuous ATO plane derives a single `authorization-state` value
from the Compliance plane's control-state, the Boundary Appendix's
boundary-conformance posture, and the Drift Detection plane's drift
classification. The derivation rule is canonical and reconstructable
from the evidence chain.

### 5.2 Mechanism 2 — Re-Assessment Triggers

Re-assessment is event-driven, not calendar-driven. The following events
are authoritative triggers:

| Trigger Class | Example |
|---|---|
| **Control-state regression** | A previously satisfied binding falls below its evidence threshold |
| **Boundary-conformance regression** | A `boundary-crossing` record indicates loss of a previously preserved signal class |
| **Schema or profile change** | A canonical schema or applicable profile is updated under the canon-change protocol |
| **Drift escalation** | A drift-record crosses a defined severity threshold |
| **Mandate update** | An applicable mandate (EO, OMB memo, NIST profile, SCuBA baseline) is updated |

Calendar-based assessments still occur where mandated externally; they
are additional, not substitutive.

### 5.3 Mechanism 3 — AO Decision-Support Surface

The AO surface presents:

1. The current `authorization-state` and its derivation trace.
2. The Tier 1 KSI set with stale/failed/conformant classification.
3. The set of open POA&M items with severity, owner, and target date.
4. The set of attributed-degradation exceptions from the Boundary
   Appendix and their threshold posture.
5. The set of pending re-assessment triggers and their disposition.

The AO is provided with the inputs that produced the
`authorization-state`, not just the conclusion. This is a non-negotiable
property of the cATO model.

### 5.4 Mechanism 4 — 3PAO Reproducibility

A 3PAO must be able to reproduce any `authorization-state` value from
the evidence chain alone, using the canonical derivation rule. This is
the operational expression of evidence-chain reconstructability (§4.1).

---

## 6. POA&M Model

Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M) records are the canonical
representation of known gaps. UIAO treats POA&M as a first-class
governance object, not a side document.

### 6.1 POA&M Lifecycle

| Stage | Producer | Consumer |
|---|---|---|
| **Open** | Compliance plane (from §2.1 step 7 gap recording) or Drift Detection (from drift-record escalation) | AO, ISSO |
| **Mitigation in progress** | Plane owner | Compliance plane, AO |
| **Compensating-control accepted** | AO via Compliance plane | 3PAO, Continuous ATO |
| **Closed** | Compliance plane, on evidence-pattern satisfaction | AO, 3PAO, Continuous ATO |
| **Aged** | Compliance plane, when target date passes without closure | AO (escalated) |

### 6.2 POA&M Closure Rule

A POA&M item closes only when:

1. The triggering gap or drift is no longer present in the evidence chain.
2. The closure event is itself a provenance-record.
3. The closure does not depend on attributed degradation exceeding the
   thresholds set in Boundary Appendix §3.2.

Closure-by-narrative is forbidden; this is the POA&M-level expression
of §4.2 forbidden evidence pattern 1.

---

## 7. Conformance Criteria

A deployment is Compliance-conforming when, and only when, all of the
following are demonstrably true:

| # | Criterion | Evidence Surface |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Every applicable control has a binding or an open POA&M item | Control library coverage report |
| 2 | Every binding satisfies the §2.2 quality bar | Binding audit |
| 3 | KSIs are derived per §3.1, with declared tiering and cadence | KSI derivation trace |
| 4 | The evidence chain satisfies all six §4.1 properties | Chain integrity scan |
| 5 | OSCAL artifacts are regenerable from the chain on demand | OSCAL regeneration test |
| 6 | The cATO authorization-state is reconstructable by a 3PAO from the chain alone | Reproducibility audit |
| 7 | POA&M items obey the §6.2 closure rule | POA&M closure audit |
| 8 | Mandate coverage from §1 is bound to planes per Phase 1 §5.3 with no orphan mandates | Mandate-coverage matrix |

A deployment that satisfies criteria 1–4 is *evidence-conforming*; one
that additionally satisfies 5–8 is *authorization-conforming*. Phase 1
§7 names this distinction; Boundary Appendix §6 reinforces it; this
document operationalizes it.

---

## 8. Risks Specific to Compliance & ATO

The following risks are tracked inside this document, in addition to the
program-level Phase 0 §7 risk register and the boundary-specific risks
in Boundary Appendix §7.

| # | Risk | Mitigation Posture |
|---|---|---|
| C1 | Control catalog drift outpaces binding refresh | Catalog ingestion (§2.1 step 1) is automated and re-run on every canonical update |
| C2 | KSIs become decorative — surface improves while underlying evidence does not | KSI derivation trace (§3.1) is auditable; Tier 1 KSI changes require provenance citation |
| C3 | OSCAL projections diverge from canonical chain | OSCAL regeneration test (§7 criterion 5) runs on every assessment cycle |
| C4 | POA&M items close on narrative rather than evidence | §6.2 closure rule enforced as automated check |
| C5 | cATO authorization-state derived from a chain with hidden boundary gaps | §5.1 derivation must consume Boundary Appendix conformance posture as input |
| C6 | Mandate updates are applied to documentation but not to bindings | Mandate-update event is an authoritative re-assessment trigger (§5.2) |

Each risk has an explicit owner in the Compliance plane and an
escalation path to the AO via the Continuous ATO surface.

---

## 9. Hand-off to the 90-Day Execution Plan

This document closes when ratified. The 90-Day Execution Plan consumes
it as follows:

- §2 (Control Binding Approach) sequences the catalog-ingestion and
  binding work as discrete Day-N activities.
- §4 (Evidence Chain Requirements) defines the acceptance bar for every
  evidence-related deliverable in the 90-day window.
- §5 (cATO Operating Model) is the target operating posture the 90-day
  window must establish, even if not yet sustained at full cadence.
- §7 (Conformance Criteria) defines the conformance gates against which
  90-day milestones are evaluated.

No 90-Day milestone may declare completion against an unratified
Compliance & ATO Alignment baseline; this is the same sequencing rule
established in Phase 0 §8 and restated throughout the master plan.

---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## Document Governance
This Compliance & ATO Alignment document is a canonical program
artifact. Changes are governed by the canon-change protocol; substantive
revisions require an ADR, a schema check, and a drift scan. The document
derives from the Phase 1 Architecture Document and the Boundary
Modernization Appendix, and is the upstream source for the 90-Day
Execution Plan.
:::
